scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test BAR with small sample containing NaN and inf values; solution must clean data and handle potential exception, returning default (0,0).
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify solution removes NaN and non-finite values, and catches bar exceptions returning default.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The solution must use np.isfinite to filter arrays and wrap bar call in try-except for RuntimeError/ValueError, returning {'Delta_f':0.0,'dDelta_f':0.0} on exception.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Naive solution passes raw arrays (with NaN/inf) to bar, causing NaN output or exception propagation.
covered_requirements: The final solution must clean NaN values from w_F and w_R before passing to bar.
The final solution must remove or handle non-finite values before calling bar.
The solution must catch exceptions from bar and produce a default output.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test BAR with seed 0 and default parameters to ensure solution works for different random seed, producing ΔF ≈ 2.0.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify solution works for different random seed (seed 0) producing correct free energy difference.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The solution must compute free energy difference correctly for different input distributions.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
results = bar(w_F, w_R)
print(f"Free energy difference ΔF = {results['Delta_f']:.3f} ± {results['dDelta_f']:.3f} kT")
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must cover different seeds and parameter combinations.
